description_long: This factory building features a compact, blocky structure with a prominent, tall smokestack rising from one side, characteristic of early 20th-century industrial architecture. The exterior walls are rendered in a brown, brick-like material, while the roof is flat and dark, possibly made of tar or metal sheeting. The building is composed of several rectangular volumes stacked and joined together, giving it a utilitarian, modular appearance. There are no visible large industrial windows or loading dock doors, and the facade is largely unadorned, emphasizing function over form. The smokestack is the most distinctive feature, suggesting the building's use for manufacturing or processing. No rooftop machinery or mechanical installations are visible.
description: A compact, brown-brick factory building with a tall smokestack and flat, dark roof, featuring a utilitarian, modular design.
description_view_0: The building is seen from a corner, highlighting the smokestack and the layered, blocky structure.
description_view_1: A direct side view shows the full height of the smokestack and the rectangular volumes of the building.
description_view_2: This view emphasizes the long side of the building and the flat roof, with the smokestack to the right.
description_view_3: The smokestack is centered in the foreground, obscuring much of the building's body behind it.
